alt: erdlens terminal showcase: a SQL schema turned into a Mermaid ER diagram, a workflow spec turned into a flowchart, and a drift check catching a stale diagram after a migration.
threads:
Minta Claude Code dokumentasiin database. Dia tulis doc-nya, kamu bikin ER diagram di tool lain, copy-paste balik. Kerja dua kali. Dan begitu ada migration, diagram di doc jadi salah tanpa ketahuan.

erdlens nutup loop itu. MCP server: Claude baca skema kamu (SQL/Prisma/Drizzle/TypeORM/SQLAlchemy) → ER diagram Mermaid → tulis langsung ke dokumen. Plus workflow → flowchart, dan drift-check yang nangkep diagram basi.

Zero-dep, MIT: https://github.com/ryanda9910/erdlens
bluesky:
Claude Code documents your DB, you draw the ER diagram elsewhere and paste it back. Double work, and after a migration it's quietly wrong.

erdlens closes the loop: an MCP server that reads your schema and writes the diagram into the doc. Plus workflows + drift.
